The search starts at a specified character position and proceeds backward toward the beginning of the string for a specified number of character positions. A parameter specifies the type of search to use.

member.name: M:Polyfills.Polyfill.GetChunks(System.Text.StringBuilder)
member.summary: GetChunks returns ChunkEnumerator that follows the IEnumerable pattern and thus can be used in a C# 'foreach' statements to retrieve the data in the StringBuilder as chunks (ReadOnlyMemory) of characters.

member.name: T:Polyfills.Polyfill.ChunkEnumerator
member.summary: ChunkEnumerator supports both the IEnumerable and IEnumerator pattern so foreach works (see GetChunks). It needs to be public (so the compiler can use it when building a foreach statement) but users typically don't use it explicitly. (which is why it is a nested type).

member.name: T:System.Threading.Lock
member.summary: Provides a way to get mutual exclusion in regions of code between different threads. A lock may be held by one thread at a time.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.Enter
member.summary: Enters the lock. Once the method returns, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.TryEnter
member.summary: Tries to enter the lock without waiting. If the lock is entered, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.TryEnter(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Tries to enter the lock, waiting for roughly the specified duration. If the lock is entered, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.TryEnter(System.Int32)
member.summary: Tries to enter the lock, waiting for roughly the specified duration. If the lock is entered, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.Exit
member.summary: Exits the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.EnterScope
member.summary: Enters the lock and returns a that may be disposed to exit the lock. Once the method returns, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock. This method is intended to be used along with a language construct that would automatically dispose the , such as with the C# using statement.

member.name: T:System.Threading.Lock.Scope
member.summary: A disposable structure that is returned by , which when disposed, exits the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.Scope.#ctor(System.Threading.Lock)
member.summary: A disposable structure that is returned by , which when disposed, exits the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.Scope.Dispose
member.summary: Exits the lock.

The section of the line to erase.

1 From the beginning of the line through the cursor.
2 The complete line.

member.summary: This control function erases characters from part or all of the display. When you erase complete lines, they become single-height, single-width lines, with all visual character attributes cleared. ED works inside or outside the scrolling margins.
member.param:
The amount of the display to erase.
0 From the cursor through the end of the display.
1 From the beginning of the display through the cursor.
2 The complete display.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.Index

member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.ReverseIndex
member.summary: Moves the cursor up one line in the same column by emitting ESC M. If the cursor is at the top margin, then the screen performs a scroll-down.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.DeleteCharacter(System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function deletes one or more characters from the cursor position to the right by emitting CSI n P.
member.param: The number of characters to delete. If is greater than the number of characters between the cursor and the right margin, then DCH only deletes the remaining characters.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.SetCursorStyle(System.Int32)
member.summary: Select the style of the cursor on the screen by emitting CSI n SP q
member.param:
The style of the cursor
0 Terminal default.
1 Blinking block.
2 Steady block.
3 Blinking underline.
5 Steady underline.
6 Steady vertical bar.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.DeleteLine(System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function deletes one or more lines in the scrolling region by emitting CSI n M, starting with the line that has the cursor. As lines are deleted, lines below the cursor and in the scrolling region move up. The terminal adds blank lines with no visual character attributes at the bottom of the scrolling region. If lines is greater than the number of lines remaining on the page, DL deletes only the remaining lines. DL has no effect outside the scrolling margins.
member.param: The number of lines to delete.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.EraseCharacter(System.Int32)
member.summary: Erases one or more characters from the cursor position to the right by emitting CSI n X (ECH). ECH clears character attributes from erased character positions. ECH works inside or outside the scrolling margins.
member.param: The number of characters to erase. A value of 0 or 1 erases one character.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.InsertCharacter(System.Int32)
member.summary: inserts one or more space (SP) characters starting at the cursor position by emitting CSI n @ (ICH). The ICH sequence inserts blank characters with the normal character attribute. The cursor remains at the beginning of the blank characters. Text between the cursor and right margin moves to the right. Characters scrolled past the right margin are lost. ICH has no effect outside the scrolling margins.
member.param: The number of characters to insert.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.InsertLine(System.Int32)
member.summary: Inserts one or more blank lines, starting at the cursor by emitting CSI n L (IL). As lines are inserted, lines below the cursor and in the scrolling region move down. Lines scrolled off the page are lost. IL has no effect outside the page margins.
member.param: The number of lines to insert.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.ScrollDown(System.Int32)
member.summary: Moves the user window up a specified number of lines in page memory by emitting CSI n T.
member.param: The number of lines to move the user window up in page memory. lines new lines appear at the top of the display. lines old lines disappear at the bottom of the display. You cannot pan past the top margin of the current page.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.ScrollUp(System.Int32)
member.summary: Moves the user window down a specified number of lines in page memory by emitting CSI n S.
member.param: The number of lines to move the user window down in page memory. lines new lines appear at the bottom of the display. lines old lines disappear at the top of the display. You cannot pan past the bottom margin of the current page.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.summary: No colors.

member.name: T:Spectre.Console.Decoration
member.summary: Represents text decoration.
member.remarks: Support for text decorations is up to the terminal.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.None
member.summary: No text decoration.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Bold
member.summary: Bold text. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Dim
member.summary: Dim or faint text. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Italic
member.summary: Italic text. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Underline
member.summary: Underlined text. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Invert
member.summary: Swaps the foreground and background colors. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Conceal
member.summary: Hides the text. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.SlowBlink
member.summary: Makes text blink. Normally less than 150 blinks per minute. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.RapidBlink
member.summary: Makes text blink. Normally more than 150 blinks per minute. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Strikethrough
member.summary: Shows text with a horizontal line through the center. Not supported in every environment.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.summary: Combines this style with another one.
member.param: The item to combine with this.

member.summary: Implicitly converts into a .

member.summary: Converts the string representation of a style to its equivalent.
member.param: A string containing a style to parse.
member.returns: A equivalent of the text contained in .

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Style.TryParse(System.String,Spectre.Console.Style@)
member.summary: Converts the string representation of a style to its equivalent. A return value indicates whether the operation succeeded.
member.param: A string containing a style to parse.
When this method returns, contains the equivalent of the text contained in , if the conversion succeeded, or null if the conversion failed.
member.returns: true if s was converted successfully; otherwise, false.

member.summary: Returns the markup representation of this style.

member.summary: Contains extension methods for .

member.summary: Creates a new style from the specified one with the specified foreground color.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.summary: Creates a new style from the specified one with the specified background color.

member.name: T:Spectre.Console.StringExtensions
member.summary: Contains extension methods for .

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.StringExtensions.EscapeMarkup(System.String)
member.summary: Escapes text so that it won’t be interpreted as markup.
member.returns: A string that is safe to use in markup.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.StringExtensions.RemoveMarkup(System.String)
member.summary: Removes markup from the specified string.
member.param: The text to remove markup from.
member.returns: A string that does not have any markup.
